sourceMovie.title: Transcendence
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Transcendence for the same reason it broke through in 2014: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a science fiction rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Wally Pfister keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The thriller and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: WarGames
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Transcendence clicked for you, WarGames (1983) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Transcendence?
faq.answer: Try WarGames, Archive, Wifelike—each shares DNA with Transcendence while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Transcendence?
faq.answer: Transcendence (2014) is best described as Thriller, Science Fiction, Drama, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Transcendence worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
